title: cyphia digitata var. tomentosa
intro: Cyphia digitata var. tomentosa, also known as the hairy finger root, is a plant species in the family Cyphiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off microbial infections. Herbal preparations of Cyphia digitata var. tomentosa include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat the mentioned health issues.
